title: plectranthus thymiflorus
intro: Plectranthus thymiflorus, also known as Mexican mint or lemon-scented coleus, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. The leaves of Plectranthus thymiflorus have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. The leaves can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
